scenario: System hangs with soft lockup and rcu_sched CPU stall warning after kernel update
symptoms: watchdog: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#7 stuck for 22s! [modprobe:3779]
rcu: INFO: rcu_sched self-detected stall on CPU

workflow.thought: Check the version of the running kernel
Check kernel ring buffer for soft lockup warnings
workflow.observation: watchdog: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#7 stuck for 22s! [modprobe:3779]

workflow.thought: Count occurrences of soft lockup warnings
workflow.action: log | grep BUG: | awk '{print $(NF-4) , $(NF)}' | sort -nr | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr

workflow.thought: Check for rcu_sched CPU stall warnings
workflow.observation: rcu: INFO: rcu_sched self-detected stall on CPU

workflow.thought: Verify the modprobe task details
Check process status with memory info
Get backtrace of the modprobe task
Identify system call being executed
Check file descriptor for module being loaded
Disassemble the spinlock acquisition function
Disassemble the _raw_spin_lock function
Search for the native_queued_spin_lock_slowpath address
Check kernel memory for pv_lock_ops structure
Get backtraces of all active tasks on each CPU
Check module taints and details
Get module information
Inspect module structure details
Check file details for symlink to oracleoks module
Inspect inode details of symlink
Check file details for actual oracleoks module
Inspect inode details of actual module file

root_cause_analysis: The fix of kABI breakage in struct pv_mmu_ops reported via private Bug ID: #2144161#2152206 has a side effect on third-party kernel modules built on top of RHEL-8.7 GA kernel version 4.18.0-425.3.1.el8.
The version of the third-party module [oracleoks] linked to the kernel version 4.18.0-425.10.1.el8_7 or higher is incompatible.
The [oracleoks] kernel module file inside 4.18.0-425.13.1.el8_7.x86_64/weak-updates/usm directory is a symlink to the [oracleoks] kernel module inside 4.18.0-425.3.1.el8.x86_64/extra/usm directory.
